title: Obas
content:

{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''WARNING'': Facilities on Obas have lost environmental control capabilities.|Message from the Obas base to a group of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Meltdown" />}}
'''Obas''' was a superheated [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Andirma sector/Legends|Andirma sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] operated a [[Obas base|base]] on the planet, the environmental control systems of which at one point malfunctioned. As a result, the base faced imminent melt-down due to the extremely high [[Temperature/Legends|temperatures]] of Obas. The personnel of the base tasked a group of Rebel agents that had arrived in the [[Obas system/Legends|Obas system]] to [[Mission to the Imperial supply depot|retrieve]] critical replacement [[KL-45 coolant hydro-valve|parts]] from an [[Imperial supply depot (Rainos Cluster)|Imperial supply depot]] in the nearby [[Rainos Cluster/Legends|Rainos Cluster]].

At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] force discovered the Obas base and dispatched a [[TL-118 "StarHammer" Short-range Attack Ship|heavy assault starfighter]] and [[Land assault trooper|Imperial Army assault troopers]] to the planet. When the Rebel agents returned to the Obas [[Star system/Legends|system]], they [[Space warfare/Legends|fought]] the Imperial [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] and subsequently entered the base on the planet. After the group had located the [[Power conduit/Legends|power conduit]] at which the newly-brought parts needed to be installed, the Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], who had also entered the complex, [[Attack at the Obas base|attacked]] the Rebels.

==Description==
{{Quote|Estimates give less than nine hours before complete melt-down.|Message from the Obas base to a group of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Meltdown" />}}
Obas was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Obas system/Legends|Obas system]], a part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]]<ref name="Meltdown" /> [[Andirma sector/Legends|Andirma sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated at a distance of only two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours']] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel from an [[Imperial supply depot (Rainos Cluster)|Imperial supply depot]] in the [[Rainos Cluster/Legends|Rainos Cluster]]. Obas occupied the second [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around its [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Oba]], and a single [[Moon/Legends|moon]] circled it in turn. The [[local year]] on the planet consisted of 401 [[Local day/Legends|local days]], each of which was 42 standard hours long.<ref name="Meltdown" />

Obas was a high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] [[Temperature/Legends|superheated]] world with high levels of [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] in its environment, a Type II [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] in which the use of a [[Breath mask/Legends|breath mask]] was recommended, and a searing hot climate that even had the potential to harm living beings in [[Environment suit/Legends|vacuum suits]]. Except for an arid [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]], the planet was predominantly a [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] wasteland, with such terrain features as [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], rocky plains, and volcanoes.<ref name="Meltdown" />

==History==
===Crisis at a Rebel base===
{{Quote|I used to own a couple of these that I 'liberated' from an Imperial depot out near Obas.|Roar Garnet, Cynabar's Droid Datalog|<ref name="Cynabar">''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]''</ref>}}
The [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]<ref name="RPG">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']]</ref> [[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]] once [[Thief/Legends|stole]] several [[Elegance message drone|''Elegance'' message drones]] from an [[Unidentified Imperial depot|Imperial depot]] near Obas.<ref name="Cynabar" /> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="1 ABY">In ''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'', the [[in-universe]] comment by [[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]] regarding the [[Thief/Legends|theft]] from the [[Unidentified Imperial depot|depot]] near Obas is dated to "36:4:3:03," which is a [[Great ReSynchronization]] date corresponding to [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> Garnet noted the fact in the [[Scandoc/Legends|scandoc]] known as [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog]].<ref name="Cynabar" />

[[File:Andirma-EAOC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Rebel Alliance established a base on Obas due to its strategic location in the Expansion Region's Andirma sector.]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] established a [[Obas base|base]] on Obas due to the planet's strategic location in the Expansion Region, which made it a perfect stopover point for Rebel [[Spy/Legends|spies]] bringing information from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] to the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. At some point during that conflict, the environmental control systems of the Obas base malfunctioned, putting the facility at risk of melting into slag and endangering the lives of the base's personnel. Several of the facility's systems shut down as a result of the planet's intense heat, and all personnel was sequestered in the base's control center, to which all the remaining power of the facility was rerouted.<ref name="Meltdown" />

When a group of Rebel agents arrived at the edge of the Obas [[Star system/Legends|system]] in a [[Starship/Legends|starship]], it received a message from the Obas base. The message informed the Rebels of the situation at the facility and instructed the group to obtain for the base thirty [[KL-45 coolant hydro-valve]]s from the nearby Rainos Cluster [[Baas-class space station|Imperial supply depot]]. If the agents would not retrieve and install the replacement [[Coolant/Legends|coolant]] hydro-valves in [[Time/Legends|time]], it was estimated that the Obas base would suffer a complete melt-down in less than nine hours.<ref name="Meltdown" />

===A race against time===
{{RPG|"[[Meltdown (adventure)|Meltdown]]"}}
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''NEW ORDERS'': Procure 30 SoroSuub KL-45 coolant hydro-valves. Best local source is Imperial supply depot in Rainos Cluster.|Message from the Obas base to a group of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Meltdown" />}}
[[File:ObasLoneScoutA-IA.png|thumb|right|180px|An Imperial scouting vessel at Obas]]
Shortly after receiving the message, the group of Rebels discovered the presence of an [[Lone Scout-A|Imperial scouting vessel]] in the Obas system. The [[Scout ship/Legends|starship]] was a part of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] force that was searching Expansion Region planets for any signs of Rebel outposts, and it was slowly circling each of the system's worlds, beginning with the outermost and working inward. At that point, the Rebel agents [[Mission to the Imperial supply depot|departed]] for the Rainos Cluster [[Space station/Legends|supply depot]], where they were ultimately successful in obtaining the coolant hydro-valves. While the Rebels were away, the Imperial scouting vessel visited the atmosphere of Obas and eventually, a modified [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] notified Imperial authorities on anomalies detected in the system.<ref name="Meltdown" />

In response, the Empire dispatched to the Obas system a [[TL-118 "StarHammer" Short-range Attack Ship]] and a civilian [[Transport/Legends|transport]] vessel carrying a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Land assault trooper|Imperial Army assault troopers]] as well as a group of [[Imperial Saboteur|Imperial saboteurs]]. The civilian transport landed at the Obas base, where the Imperials forced the main [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]] doors open, allowing the assault [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] to begin searching the complex. The heavy assault [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], however, remained in Obas's orbit, where it engaged the Rebel agents' starship in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] upon the latter's return from the Rainos Cluster.<ref name="Meltdown" />

After defeating the fighter, the Rebels' ship descended into Obas's lower troposphere, which allowed the agents to make visual contact with the entrance of the Rebel base. The group entered the facility and eventually located its control center, whereupon one of the base's [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] instructed the agents to install the coolant hydro-valves at a [[Power conduit/Legends|power conduit]] in the facility's maintenance area. When the group finally located the power conduit, it was [[Attack at the Obas base|attacked]] by the Imperial assault troopers, who had also entered the domed chamber. At the same time, a stressed section of the chamber's ceiling began to crack due to the heat of the outside and eventually crashed down on the maintenance area's floor.<ref name="Meltdown" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|All personnel have been sequestered in the control center.|Message from the Obas base to a group of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Meltdown" />}}
The superheated Obas had no native [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Other than in the Rebel Alliance base on its surface, the planet had no form of government. The base's population numbered sixty-two individuals, and no goods of note were exported from the planet, since the base's primary value was in its strategic location. Conversely, agricultural products, high technology, and medical supplies were all imported on Obas in order for the base's existence to continue in the face of the adverse conditions of the planet.<ref name="Meltdown" />

==Locations==
{{Main|Obas base}}
{{Quote|Several systems have shut down completely, including sensors, internal comm, and docking bay main access-way.|Message from the Obas base to a group of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Meltdown" />}}
The Rebel Alliance constructed a base with limited docking facilities inside a cliff on Obas. The hostile conditions of the planet necessitated the use of several protective measures, such as massive doors that kept the heat out of the facility's main docking bay and the use of coolant throughout the base's systems. The docking area of the Obas base housed twelve [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]], twenty-four [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]], and fifteen [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]].<ref name="Meltdown" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Instant_Adventures.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Obas was introduced in the 1997 book ''Instant Adventures''.]]
Obas served as a setting for "[[Meltdown (adventure)|Meltdown]]," a [[roleplaying game]] adventure authored by [[George R. Strayton]], illustrated by [[Mike Chen (illustrator)|Mike Chen]] and included in the [[1997]] supplement ''[[Instant Adventures]]'' for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Meltdown" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Obas system, and therefore the planet Obas, in grid square L-15.<ref name="TEA" />

"Meltdown" suggests that the player characters can be temporarily reassigned to the Rebel Alliance base on Obas, or they can be permanently stationed there and at the beginning of the adventure can be returning from a delivery mission to the Outer Rim Territories, during which they have had no contact with the base. In addition, the adventure lists several possible outcomes for the characters' mission to install the replacement coolant hydro-valves at the base. If they fail the task, they have to help the base's personnel escape the destruction of the facility.<ref name="Meltdown" />

If the player characters manage to help restore full power to the Obas base, they can send a forged report to the Empire indicating that the base has been abandoned and then use the civilian transport used by the Imperial force as a distraction while the facility is evacuated. Alternatively, the characters can learn from the Imperial assault troopers they fought against that a picket patrol of two [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruisers]] is on its way to investigate the Rebel presence on Obas, and the base must be defended by its own starfighter complement. Instead of a threat of an immediate military response from the Empire, the gamemaster player can have the other players deal with the presence of hidden Imperial [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]] in the base. Finally, if the player characters save the Obas base from destruction and prevent its detection by the wider Empire, they can use it as a launching point for missions in further adventures.<ref name="Meltdown" />
